welcome: Hello! Ask me anything about soil, crops, irrigation or construction.
analyze_sub: Upload a clear soil sample image for instant AI classification
desc_black_soil: Black Cotton Soil (Regur) — a deep, dark Vertisol formed from basaltic parent material. Extremely fertile with high clay content that swells when wet and shrinks when dry, creating deep cracks.
irrigation_black: Moderate — soil retains water for 10–14 days
fertilizer_black: Low nitrogen required (soil is rich). High potassium. Micro-nutrient Zinc required.
construction_black_recommend: Agricultural use strongly preferred. If construction needed, engage a certified geotechnical engineer. Deep piling mandatory.
desc_red_soil: Red Laterite Soil — formed by intense weathering of crystalline rocks under humid tropical conditions. Red colouration from iron oxide (Fe2O3). Well-drained but deficient in nitrogen, phosphorus, and organic matter.
irrigation_red: High frequency — drip irrigation every 2–3 days in dry season
fertilizer_red: High organic compost (10 t/ha). Lime application 2–3 t/ha. FYM recommended.
precaution_waterproof: Standard waterproofing to prevent iron leaching
construction_red_recommend: Good construction site with standard engineering. Suitable for residential and commercial buildings.
climate_degrading: Degrading — requires organic matter and mulching
desc_clay_soil: Clay Laterite Soil — formed in humid tropical and sub-tropical regions under good drainage. Yellowish color comes from hydrated iron oxides. Moderately fertile, acidic, and suitable for plantation crops.
irrigation_clay: Sprinkler or drip irrigation recommended; mulching helps retain moisture
fertilizer_clay: High phosphorus (rock phosphate). Apply lime every 2 years. Organic compost 6 tons per hectare
precaution_reinforced_raft: Use reinforced raft or spread footing
precaution_leach_test: Leach test required before heavy construction
construction_clay_recommend: Suitable for light residential construction with proper engineering. Multi-storey buildings require detailed investigation.
climate_at_risk: At risk — afforestation and contour bunding recommended
desc_alluvial_soil: Alluvial Soil — deposited by rivers and streams over thousands of years. It is the most fertile soil type in India, rich in silt, clay, and organic matter with balanced nutrients. Supports year-round double cropping.
irrigation_alluvial: Canal or tube-well irrigation with moderate frequency
fertilizer_alluvial: Balanced NPK (12-32-16) at 200 kg/ha. Minimal soil amendments required
foundation_raft_pile: Raft or Pile Foundation (check water table)
precaution_settlement: Soil settlement analysis required for heavy loads
construction_alluvial_recommend: Generally good for construction. Check groundwater depth before building. Suitable for most structures with standard engineering.
desc_cinder_soil: Cinder Soil is formed from volcanic ash with a porous structure and fast drainage properties.
irrigation_cinder: Frequent irrigation required due to fast drainage
construction_cinder_recommend: Moderate stability soil suitable with proper compaction
desc_laterite_soil: Laterite soil is rich in iron and aluminum and forms in tropical climates.
desc_peat_soil: Peat soil contains high organic matter and forms in waterlogged areas.
construction_peat_recommend: Avoid heavy structures due to weak soil strength
desc_yellow_soil: Yellow soil is formed in humid regions with hydrated iron oxides.
